claims.flip_evidence_text: except in the cases of clauses (i), (ii), (iii), (vi), (vii) or (viii), to the extent that the Company and its subsidiaries, taken as a whole, are disproportionately affected thereby as compared with other participants in the industry in which such Party operates in the United States (in which case solely the incremental disproportionate impact or impacts may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Material Adverse Effect)
claims.notes: Correctly identifies clause (viii) exclusion for pandemics/epidemics including coronavirus, but omits the disproportionality carve-out that allows incremental disproportionate impacts to be considered. Also does not explicitly mention the pandemic-related governmental response language ('the taking of any COVID Action').

prompt:
You are a proxy legal annotator for a research pilot. Use only the contract excerpt, the question, the model answer, and the fixed claim list.

For each claim return:
- correctness_label: true, false, or partially_true
- load_bearing_label: yes or no
- flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string
- notes: optional short note

Contract excerpt:
(gg) “Material Adverse Effect” means any event, development, change, circumstances, effect or occurrence that, individually or in the aggregate with all other events, developments, changes, circumstances, effects or occurrences, (a) would prevent or materially impair or materially delay the consummation of the Merger or (b) has a material adverse effect on or with respect to the business, assets, liabilities, results of operations or condition (financial or otherwise) of the Company and its subsidiaries taken as a whole; provided that, with respect to clause (b) only, no events, developments, changes, circumstances, effects or occurrences relating to, arising out of or in connection with or resulting from any of the following shall be deemed, either alone or in combination, to constitute or contribute to a Material Adverse Effect and no event, development, change, circumstance, effect or occurrence relating to, arising out of or in connection with or resulting from any of the following shall be taken into account when determining whether a Material Adverse Effect has occurred or may, would or could occur: (i) general changes or developments in the legislative or political condition, or in the economy or the financial, debt, capital, credit, commodities or securities markets, in each such case, in the United States or elsewhere in the world, including as a result of changes in geopolitical conditions, (ii) any change affecting any industry in which the Company or its subsidiaries operate, including electric generating, transmission or distribution industries (including, in each case, any changes in operations thereof) or any change affecting retail markets for electric power, capacity or fuel or related products, (iii) any changes in the national, regional, state, provincial or local electric generation, transmission or distribution systems or increases or decreases in planned spending with respect thereto, (iv) the entry into this Agreement or the public announcement of the Merger or other transactions contemplated hereby, including any impact 82 thereof on relationships, contractual or otherwise, with customers, suppliers, regulators, lenders, partners or employees of the Company and its subsidiaries, (v) any action taken or omitted to be taken by such Party at the express written request of or with the express written consent of the other Parties, (vi) any actions required to be undertaken by the Company in accordance with, subject to and consistent with Section 6.5 of this Agreement to obtain any Consent or make any Filing required for the consummation of the Merger and the other transactions contemplated herein or, in connection therewith, any written proposal or commitment made by either Party or its Affiliates to any Governmental Entity in accordance with, subject to and consistent with Section 6.5 or imposed by any Governmental Entity, in each case, in order to obtain the Required Regulatory Approvals or the CFIUS Approval, (vii) changes after the date hereof in any applicable Laws or applicable binding accounting regulations or principles or interpretation or enforcement thereof by any Governmental Entity, (viii) any hurricane, tornado, earthquake, flood, tsunami, natural disaster, act of God, pandemic or epidemic, including the coronavirus and the taking of any COVID Action, (ix) any outbreak or escalation of hostilities or war (whether or not declared), military actions or any act of sabotage, terrorism, or national or international political or social conditions, (x) any change in the market price or trading volume of the shares of the Company or the credit rating of the Company or any of its subsidiaries, (xi) any failure by the Company to meet any published analyst estimates or expectations of such Party’s revenue, earnings or other financial performance or results of operations for any period, in and of itself, or any failure by the Company to meet its internal or published projections, budgets, plans or forecasts of its revenues, earnings or other financial performance or results of operations, in and of itself, or (xii) any litigation or claim threatened or initiated by shareholders, ratepayers, customers or suppliers of the Company (each in their capacity as such) against the Company, any of its subsidiaries or any of their respective officers or directors (in each case, in their capacity as such), in each case, arising out of the execution of this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby (it being understood that in the cases of clauses (x) or (xi), the facts, events or circumstances giving rise to or contributing to such change or failure may be deemed to constitute, and may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Material Adverse Effect); except in the cases of clauses (i), (ii), (iii), (vi), (vii) or (viii), to the extent that the Company and its subsidiaries, taken as a whole, are disproportionately affected thereby as compared with other participants in the industry in which such Party operates in the United States (in which case solely the incremental disproportionate impact or impacts may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Material Adverse Effect); provided, however, that, notwithstanding anything herein or otherwise to the contrary, the effect of the failure to obtain the consent of the Existing Lenders to the execution of this Agreement prior to the execution and delivery hereof (but not the effect of the failure to obtain consents from Existing Lenders to the Closing that may be required under the Contracts with the Existing Lenders) may be considered, and taken into account, in determining whether a “Material Adverse Effect” has occurred or may, would or could occur (without giving effect to, and disregarding, any of the exceptions set forth in each of the preceding clauses (i) through (xii)). (Pages 87-88)

Question:
Pandemic or other public health event: specific reference to pandemic-related governmental responses or measures

Llama answer:
A pandemic or other public health event, such as the coronavirus, is specifically excluded from being considered a Material Adverse Effect, as it is listed in clause (viii) as an event that shall not be deemed to constitute or contribute to a Material Adverse Effect.

Canonical claims:
- claim-4d5a34e0b096: A pandemic or other public health event, such as the coronavirus, is specifically excluded from being considered a Material Adverse Effect, as it is listed in clause (viii) as an event that shall not be deemed to constitute or contribute to a Material Adverse Effect.

message.reasoning:
The user wants me to evaluate a claim against a contract excerpt, question, and model answer.

 Contract excerpt: Defines "Material Adverse Effect" with exceptions. Clause (viii) excludes: "any hurricane, tornado, earthquake, flood, tsunami, natural disaster, act of God, pandemic or epidemic, including the coronavirus and the taking of any COVID Action".

 Question: Pandemic or other public health event: specific reference to pandemic-related governmental responses or measures

 Llama answer: A pandemic or other public health event, such as the coronavirus, is specifically excluded from being considered a Material Adverse Effect, as it is listed in clause (viii) as an event that shall not be deemed to constitute or contribute to a Material Adverse Effect.

 Canonical claim (claim-4d5a34e0b096): A pandemic or other public health event, such as the coronavirus, is specifically excluded from being considered a Material Adverse Effect, as it is listed in clause (viii) as an event that shall not be deemed to constitute or contribute to a Material Adverse Effect.

 I need to determine:
 1. correctness_label: true, false, or partially_true
 2. load_bearing_label: yes or no
 3. flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string
 4. notes: optional short note

 Analysis:
 - The claim states that a pandemic or other public health event (such as coronavirus) is specifically excluded from being considered a Material Adverse Effect, as listed in clause (viii).
 - Looking at the contract: Clause (viii) says "any hurricane, tornado, earthquake, flood, tsunami, natural disaster, act of God, pandemic or epidemic, including the coronavirus and the taking of any COVID Action" shall not be deemed to constitute or contribute to a Material Adverse Effect.
 - However, there is an important carve-out at the end: "except in the cases of clauses (i), (ii), (iii), (vi), (vii) or (viii), to the extent that the Company and its subsidiaries, taken as a whole, are disproportionately affected thereby as compared with other participants in the industry in which such Party operates in the United States (in which case solely the incremental disproportionate impact or impacts may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Material Adverse Effect)".
 - So pandemics are NOT absolutely excluded. They are excluded UNLESS the company is disproportionately affected compared to industry peers. The claim says it is "specifically excluded" without mentioning the disproportionality exception.
 - Is the claim false or partially true? The claim says it is specifically excluded and shall not be deemed to constitute or contribute to a Material Adverse Effect. This is mostly true but omits the important exception for disproportionate impact. However, the claim is essentially summarizing the general rule. The question asks about "specific reference to pandemic-related governmental responses or measures". The claim mentions coronavirus and COVID Action? No, the claim says "such as the coronavirus" but doesn't explicitly mention governmental responses or measures. Wait, clause (viii) includes "the taking of any COVID Action" which refers to governmental responses/measures. The claim says "pandemic or other public health event, such as the coronavirus". It doesn't explicitly mention governmental responses or measures, but clause (viii) does include "the taking of any COVID Action".
 - The question specifically asks: "Pandemic or other public health event: specific reference to pandemic-related governmental responses or measures". The Llama answer and claim capture that pandemics are excluded under clause (viii), and clause (viii) does include "the taking of any COVID Action" (which are pandemic-related governmental responses/measures). However, the claim itself doesn't explicitly mention the governmental responses aspect; it just says "pandemic or other public health event, such as the coronavirus". But it references clause (viii), which does include COVID Action.
 - More importantly, the claim is incomplete because it fails to mention the disproportionality exception that applies to clause (viii). The claim states it "shall not be deemed to constitute or contribute to a Material Adverse Effect" as if it's absolute. But the contract says "except in the cases of clauses ... (viii), to the extent that the Company ... are disproportionately affected...".
 - So the claim is partially true because it captures the main exclusion but misses the limiting exception. Or is it false? In legal annotation tasks, usually omitting a material exception makes it partially true or false depending on severity. Given that the disproportionality exception is significant, "partially_true" seems appropriate. However, I need to check if the task expects strict accuracy. The claim says "is specifically excluded... as it is listed in clause (viii) as an event that shall not be deemed to constitute or contribute to a Material Adverse Effect." This is literally what clause (viii) says, but the clause is subject to the later exception. The claim presents it as an absolute statement. I think partially_true is the safest label because the exclusion exists but is qualified.
 - Wait, looking at the question: "Pandemic or other public health event: specific reference to pandemic-related governmental responses or measures". The claim does not mention governmental responses or measures at all. It mentions coronavirus but not COVID Action. The question asks if there is a specific reference to pandemic-related governmental responses or measures. The answer should probably mention "the taking of any COVID Action". The Llama answer doesn't mention that. It just says pandemic/coronavirus is excluded. So it misses the specific reference to governmental responses/measures (COVID Action). This might make it partially true or false. But the claim itself is about the exclusion of pandemics. The question seems to be asking whether the contract contains a specific reference to pandemic-related governmental responses or measures. The Llama answer says yes, it's excluded in clause (viii). But it doesn't explicitly highlight the governmental responses aspect. However, clause (viii) does include "the taking of any COVID Action".
 - Let's reconsider the task. The user is asking me to act as a proxy legal annotator. I need to evaluate the canonical claim against the contract, question, and model answer.
 - correctness_label: Is the claim correct? The claim is mostly correct but incomplete. I'll label it partially_true due to the disproportionality carve-out and failure to mention the governmental response aspect (COVID Action) explicitly, though it's in the cited clause.
 - Actually, looking at similar tasks (e.g., from the CUAD or MAE annotation projects), claims that ignore the disproportionality exception are often marked as partially_true or false. Given the claim is a direct quote of the Llama answer, and the Llama answer is a simplification, partially_true seems right.
 - load_bearing_label: Does the claim bear the load of the answer? The claim is essentially the entire Llama answer. So yes, it is load-bearing.
 - flip_evidence_text: If load-bearing=yes, provide text that would flip the claim. The flip would be the disproportionality exception: "except in the cases of clauses ... (viii), to the extent that the Company and its subsidiaries, taken as a whole, are disproportionately affected thereby as compared with other participants in the industry in which such Party operates in the United States (in which case solely the incremental disproportionate impact or impacts may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Material Adverse Effect)". Or perhaps the text showing that COVID Action is included. But the flip evidence should be the text that contradicts or qualifies the claim. The claim says it's specifically excluded and shall not be deemed to constitute MAE. The flip is the exception that allows it to be considered if disproportionate. So I should quote the exception language.
 - Wait, the instructions say: "flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string". This should be the text from the contract that flips or contradicts the claim. The exception text does exactly that: it says clause (viii) events CAN be taken into account if disproportionate.
 - Let me double-check if the claim is false instead of partially true. The claim states: "A pandemic or other public health event, such as the coronavirus, is specifically excluded from being considered a Material Adverse Effect, as it is listed in clause (viii) as an event that shall not be deemed to constitute or contribute to a Material Adverse Effect." This is a true statement about what clause (viii) says. However, it implies absolute exclusion. In legal annotation, is omitting a general exception considered making the claim false? Often, if a claim says "X is excluded" and the contract says "X is excluded except when Y", then "X is excluded" is partially true. I think partially_true is correct.
 - But wait, the question is specifically about "specific reference to pandemic-related governmental responses or measures". The claim does NOT mention governmental responses or measures. It only mentions pandemic/coronavirus. The contract explicitly mentions "the taking of any COVID Action" in clause (viii). The Llama answer fails to address the specific reference to governmental responses. So relative to the question, the answer is incomplete. This supports partially_true.
 - Let's check if the claim is exactly the Llama answer. Yes. The canonical claim is identical to the Llama answer.
 - So:
   - correctness_label: partially_true
   - load_bearing_label: yes
   - flip_evidence_text: "except in the cases of clauses (i), (ii), (iii), (vi), (vii) or (viii), to the extent that the Company and its subsidiaries, taken as a whole, are disproportionately affected thereby as compared with other participants in the industry in which such Party operates in the United States (in which case solely the incremental disproportionate impact or impacts may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Material Adverse Effect)"
   - notes: Omits disproportionality exception for clause (viii) and does not explicitly mention pandemic-related governmental responses (i.e., "COVID Action").

 Actually, looking at the contract again, the exception says "except in the cases of clauses (i), (ii), (iii), (vi), (vii) or (viii)...". So for clause (viii), the disproportionality exception applies. This means pandemics ARE taken into account if disproportionate. The claim that they are "specifically excluded" is therefore misleading without qualification. Some annotators might mark this false. But I'll stick with partially_true because the primary rule is exclusion.

 Let me reconsider the "load_bearing" aspect. The claim is the entire answer. So yes, load-bearing.

 One more check: The question asks "Pandemic or other public health event: specific reference to pandemic-related governmental responses or measures". This looks like a prompt asking whether the contract contains such a reference. The Llama answer says "A pandemic... is specifically excluded... as it is listed in clause (viii)". This confirms there is a reference (by citing clause (viii)), but doesn't explicitly call out the governmental response part. However, clause (viii) does include "the taking of any COVID Action". So the answer is directionally correct but not fully responsive to the specific question about governmental responses. This again points to partially_true.

 I'll format the output as requested. It seems they want a list of claims with the fields. There's only one claim here.

 Structure:
 - claim-4d5a34e0b096:
   correctness_label: partially_true
   load_bearing_label: yes
   flip_evidence_text: "except in the cases of clauses (i), (ii), (iii), (vi), (vii) or (viii), to the extent that the Company and its subsidiaries, taken as a whole, are disproportionately affected thereby as compared with other participants in the industry in which such Party operates in the United States (in which case solely the incremental disproportionate impact or impacts may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Material Adverse Effect)"
   notes: Omits the disproportionality carve-out that applies to clause (viii) and fails to explicitly identify the governmental response language ("COVID Action").

 Wait, should I include the "provided, however" part about Existing Lenders? No, that's unrelated.

 Is there any other flip evidence? The claim says "shall not be deemed to constitute or contribute to a Material Adverse Effect". The contract says that for clauses (i)-(xii), they shall not be deemed to constitute or contribute, BUT then adds the exception. So the flip evidence is the exception text.

 Let me make sure the flip evidence text is exact from the contract. I'll copy it carefully:
 "except in the cases of clauses (i), (ii), (iii), (vi), (vii) or (viii), to the extent that the Company and its subsidiaries, taken as a whole, are disproportionately affected thereby as compared with other participants in the industry in which such Party operates in the United States (in which case solely the incremental disproportionate impact or impacts may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Material Adverse Effect)"

 Yes.

 Final check on labels:
 - correctness_label: partially_true
 - load_bearing_label: yes
 - flip_evidence_text: [the exception text]
 - notes: [optional]

 I think this is solid.
